changes.value:
## What

Virtual tags should have a restricted context menu — users cannot cut, copy, paste, delete, archive, or remove virtual tags. Only \"Inspect Tag\" should remain.

There are two levels of restriction:
1. **Tag entity context menu** (tag.yaml commands): cut/copy/archive should be suppressed for virtual tags
2. **Task-level untag command** (MentionPill's extra `task.untag` command): should be suppressed for virtual tags on tasks

**Files to modify:**
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx` — in `MentionPill`:
  - Check if resolved entity has `virtual: true`
  - If virtual, do NOT add the `task.untag` extra command
  - Pass a `virtual` flag to `useEntityCommands` or filter commands after
- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-commands.ts` — in `useEntityCommands`:
  - Accept optional `filterCommandIds?: string[]` or check entity's `virtual` field
  - When entity is virtual, filter out `entity.copy`, `entity.cut`, `entity.archive` commands
  - Keep only `ui.inspect`

Alternative: filter in `MentionPill` after `useEntityCommands` returns, before passing to `FocusScope`. This is simpler and doesn't require changing the hook signature.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Right-click on virtual tag pill shows only \"Inspect Tag\"
- [ ] \"Remove Tag\", \"Cut Tag\", \"Copy Tag\", \"Archive Tag\" are NOT shown for virtual tags
- [ ] Regular tags still show full context menu (no regression)
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts for cut/copy don't fire on virtual tag pills

## Tests
- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag has only inspect in commands
- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag has full command set
- [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

#virtual-tags
VT-10: Restrict context menu for virtual tags

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,33 +1,41 @@
 ## What

-Virtual tags should have a restricted context menu — users cannot cut, copy, paste, delete, archive, or remove virtual tags. Only \"Inspect Tag\" should remain.
+Virtual tags are read-only — users cannot cut, copy, paste, delete, archive, or remove them. This card covers both frontend context menus AND backend command protection.

-There are two levels of restriction:
-1. **Tag entity context menu** (tag.yaml commands): cut/copy/archive should be suppressed for virtual tags
-2. **Task-level untag command** (MentionPill's extra `task.untag` command): should be suppressed for virtual tags on tasks
+### Frontend: context menu restriction
+With the three-field architecture, virtual tag pills appear from the `virtual_tags` badge-list field. The `MentionPill` component can check `entity.fields.virtual === true` and:
+1. Do NOT add `task.untag` extra command (virtual tags aren't in the body to remove)
+2. Filter out `entity.copy`, `entity.cut`, `entity.archive` from schema commands
+3. Keep only `ui.inspect`

 **Files to modify:**
 - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx` — in `MentionPill`:
-  - Check if resolved entity has `virtual: true`
-  - If virtual, do NOT add the `task.untag` extra command
-  - Pass a `virtual` flag to `useEntityCommands` or filter commands after
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-commands.ts` — in `useEntityCommands`:
-  - Accept optional `filterCommandIds?: string[]` or check entity's `virtual` field
-  - When entity is virtual, filter out `entity.copy`, `entity.cut`, `entity.archive` commands
-  - Keep only `ui.inspect`
+  - After resolving entity, check if virtual
+  - If virtual: skip `extraCommands` (no task.untag), filter commands after `useEntityCommands` to only `ui.inspect`
+
+### Backend: reject tag/untag for virtual tags
+The `tag task` and `untag task` commands should reject attempts to add/remove virtual tag slugs from task bodies.

-Alternative: filter in `MentionPill` after `useEntityCommands` returns, before passing to `FocusScope`. This is simpler and doesn't require changing the hook signature.
+**Files to modify:**
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/tag.rs` — in `TagTask::execute()`, look up the tag by name; if it has `virtual: true`, return error
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/untag.rs` — same check in `UntagTask::execute()`
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/tag/delete.rs` — prevent deleting virtual tags
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/tag/cut.rs` — prevent cutting virtual tags

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Right-click on virtual tag pill shows only \"Inspect Tag\"
-- [ ] \"Remove Tag\", \"Cut Tag\", \"Copy Tag\", \"Archive Tag\" are NOT shown for virtual tags
+- [ ] Right-click on virtual tag pill shows only "Inspect Tag"
+- [ ] "Remove Tag", "Cut Tag", "Copy Tag", "Archive Tag" NOT shown for virtual tags
 - [ ] Regular tags still show full context menu (no regression)
-- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts for cut/copy don't fire on virtual tag pills
+- [ ] `tag task` with virtual tag slug returns error
+- [ ] `untag task` with virtual tag slug returns error
+- [ ] `delete tag` with virtual tag returns error
+- [ ] `cut tag` with virtual tag returns error

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag has only inspect in commands
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag has full command set
-- [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/tag.rs` — test that tagging with virtual tag errors
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/untag.rs` — test that untagging virtual tag errors
+- [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` pass

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,41 +1,27 @@
 ## What

-Virtual tags are read-only — users cannot cut, copy, paste, delete, archive, or remove them. This card covers both frontend context menus AND backend command protection.
-
-### Frontend: context menu restriction
-With the three-field architecture, virtual tag pills appear from the `virtual_tags` badge-list field. The `MentionPill` component can check `entity.fields.virtual === true` and:
-1. Do NOT add `task.untag` extra command (virtual tags aren't in the body to remove)
-2. Filter out `entity.copy`, `entity.cut`, `entity.archive` from schema commands
-3. Keep only `ui.inspect`
+Virtual tag pills should have no context menu commands except Inspect. With Option B this simplifies considerably — virtual tags aren't entities, so there ARE no entity commands to suppress. The only question is what commands, if any, to show.

-**Files to modify:**
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx` — in `MentionPill`:
-  - After resolving entity, check if virtual
-  - If virtual: skip `extraCommands` (no task.untag), filter commands after `useEntityCommands` to only `ui.inspect`
+Since virtual tags aren't entities, `useEntityCommands` won't find schema commands for them. The `MentionPill` component currently builds commands from the entity's type schema. For virtual tags (resolved from metadata, not entity store), there's no entity type to look up commands for.

-### Backend: reject tag/untag for virtual tags
-The `tag task` and `untag task` commands should reject attempts to add/remove virtual tag slugs from task bodies.
+**Approach**: When `MentionPill` resolves a tag from virtual metadata (isVirtual from VT-9), skip `useEntityCommands` entirely. Optionally provide a single "What is this?" tooltip-style inspect that shows the virtual tag description.

 **Files to modify:**
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/tag.rs` — in `TagTask::execute()`, look up the tag by name; if it has `virtual: true`, return error
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/untag.rs` — same check in `UntagTask::execute()`
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/tag/delete.rs` — prevent deleting virtual tags
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/tag/cut.rs` — prevent cutting virtual tags
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx`:
+  - When `isVirtual`: don't call `useEntityCommands`, don't add `task.untag` extra command
+  - Pass empty commands (or inspect-only) to `FocusScope`
+
+Since virtual tags aren't entities, there's nothing to cut/copy/paste/delete/archive. The problem solves itself.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Right-click on virtual tag pill shows only "Inspect Tag"
-- [ ] "Remove Tag", "Cut Tag", "Copy Tag", "Archive Tag" NOT shown for virtual tags
-- [ ] Regular tags still show full context menu (no regression)
-- [ ] `tag task` with virtual tag slug returns error
-- [ ] `untag task` with virtual tag slug returns error
-- [ ] `delete tag` with virtual tag returns error
-- [ ] `cut tag` with virtual tag returns error
+- [ ] Right-click on virtual tag pill shows no destructive commands
+- [ ] Regular tag pills still show full context menu (no regression)
+- [ ] No `task.untag` command for virtual tags (can't remove computed tags)

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag has only inspect in commands
-- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/tag.rs` — test that tagging with virtual tag errors
-- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/task/untag.rs` — test that untagging virtual tag errors
-- [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` and `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` pass
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag pill has no/minimal commands
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag pill has full command set
+- [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,26 +1,37 @@
 ## What

-Virtual tag pills should have no context menu commands except Inspect. With Option B this simplifies considerably — virtual tags aren't entities, so there ARE no entity commands to suppress. The only question is what commands, if any, to show.
+Virtual tag pills need their own context menu commands — not entity commands (they're not entities) but strategy-declared commands from the registry. This follows the same pattern as view commands: declarative definitions from the backend, execute handlers wired on the frontend.

-Since virtual tags aren't entities, `useEntityCommands` won't find schema commands for them. The `MentionPill` component currently builds commands from the entity's type schema. For virtual tags (resolved from metadata, not entity store), there's no entity type to look up commands for.
+### Frontend command wiring

-**Approach**: When `MentionPill` resolves a tag from virtual metadata (isVirtual from VT-9), skip `useEntityCommands` entirely. Optionally provide a single "What is this?" tooltip-style inspect that shows the virtual tag description.
+When `MentionPill` resolves a tag from virtual metadata (`isVirtual = true` from VT-9), it:
+1. Does NOT call `useEntityCommands` (no entity, no entity commands)
+2. Does NOT add `task.untag` extra command (virtual tags can't be removed)
+3. Reads `commands` from the `VirtualTagMeta` (provided by VT-3 context)
+4. Builds `CommandDef[]` from those commands, with execute handlers that dispatch to the backend via `backendDispatch`
+5. Passes these to `FocusScope`
+
+The execute handler for each virtual tag command dispatches to the backend like any entity command — `backendDispatch({ cmd: command.id, target: taskMoniker, ... })`. The backend command handler implements the actual logic (e.g. "Start Working" moves the task).

 **Files to modify:**
 - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx`:
-  - When `isVirtual`: don't call `useEntityCommands`, don't add `task.untag` extra command
-  - Pass empty commands (or inspect-only) to `FocusScope`
+  - When `isVirtual`: build `CommandDef[]` from `VirtualTagMeta.commands` instead of entity commands
+  - Wire execute handlers via `backendDispatch` targeting the **task** (not the tag — virtual tags aren't entities)
+  - The `taskId` prop (already on MentionPill) provides the target

-Since virtual tags aren't entities, there's nothing to cut/copy/paste/delete/archive. The problem solves itself.
+**Note**: The actual backend command handlers for virtual tag actions (e.g. `vtag.ready.start`) are implemented in the individual strategy cards (VT-5/6/7). This card only wires the frontend dispatch.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Right-click on virtual tag pill shows no destructive commands
-- [ ] Regular tag pills still show full context menu (no regression)
-- [ ] No `task.untag` command for virtual tags (can't remove computed tags)
+- [ ] Right-click on virtual tag pill shows strategy-declared commands
+- [ ] Commands dispatch to backend with task as target
+- [ ] No entity commands (cut/copy/paste/archive) shown
+- [ ] No `task.untag` shown
+- [ ] Regular tag pills still show full entity context menu (no regression)

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag pill has no/minimal commands
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag pill has full command set
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag pill shows strategy commands
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag pill has no entity commands
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag pill still shows entity commands
 - [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes

 ## Workflow

 ## What

-Virtual tag pills need their own context menu commands — not entity commands (they're not entities) but strategy-declared commands from the registry. This follows the same pattern as view commands: declarative definitions from the backend, execute handlers wired on the frontend.
-
-### Frontend command wiring
+Virtual tag pills should have no context menu commands except Inspect. With Option B this simplifies considerably — virtual tags aren't entities, so there ARE no entity commands to suppress. The only question is what commands, if any, to show.

-When `MentionPill` resolves a tag from virtual metadata (`isVirtual = true` from VT-9), it:

+Since virtual tags aren't entities, `useEntityCommands` won't find schema commands for them. The `MentionPill` component currently builds commands from the entity's type schema. For virtual tags (resolved from metadata, not entity store), there's no entity type to look up commands for.

-The execute handler for each virtual tag command dispatches to the backend like any entity command — `backendDispatch({ cmd: command.id, target: taskMoniker, ... })`. The backend command handler implements the actual logic (e.g. "Start Working" moves the task).
+**Approach**: When `MentionPill` resolves a tag from virtual metadata (isVirtual from VT-9), skip `useEntityCommands` entirely. Optionally provide a single "What is this?" tooltip-style inspect that shows the virtual tag description.

 **Files to modify:**
 - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx`:
-  - When `isVirtual`: build `CommandDef[]` from `VirtualTagMeta.commands` instead of entity commands
-  - Wire execute handlers via `backendDispatch` targeting the **task** (not the tag — virtual tags aren't entities)
-  - The `taskId` prop (already on MentionPill) provides the target
+  - When `isVirtual`: don't call `useEntityCommands`, don't add `task.untag` extra command

-**Note**: The actual backend command handlers for virtual tag actions (e.g. `vtag.ready.start`) are implemented in the individual strategy cards (VT-5/6/7). This card only wires the frontend dispatch.

-- [ ] Commands dispatch to backend with task as target
-- [ ] No entity commands (cut/copy/paste/archive) shown

-- [ ] Regular tag pills still show full entity context menu (no regression)

-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag pill has no entity commands
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag pill still shows entity commands
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that virtual tag pill has no/minimal commands
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.test.tsx` — test that regular tag pill has full command set
 - [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app-ui test` passes

changes.old_value:
## What

Virtual tag pills need their own context menu commands — not entity commands (they're not entities) but strategy-declared commands from the registry. This follows the same pattern as view commands: declarative definitions from the backend, execute handlers wired on the frontend.

### Frontend command wiring

When `MentionPill` resolves a tag from virtual metadata (`isVirtual = true` from VT-9), it:
1. Does NOT call `useEntityCommands` (no entity, no entity commands)
2. Does NOT add `task.untag` extra command (virtual tags can't be removed)
3. Reads `commands` from the `VirtualTagMeta` (provided by VT-3 context)
4. Builds `CommandDef[]` from those commands, with execute handlers that dispatch to the backend via `backendDispatch`
5. Passes these to `FocusScope`

The execute handler for each virtual tag command dispatches to the backend like any entity command — `backendDispatch({ cmd: command.id, target: taskMoniker, ... })`. The backend command handler implements the actual logic (e.g. "Start Working" moves the task).

**Files to modify:**
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/mention-pill.tsx`:
  - When `isVirtual`: build `CommandDef[]` from `VirtualTagMeta.commands` instead of entity commands
  - Wire execute handlers via `backendDispatch` targeting the **task** (not the tag — virtual tags aren't entities)
  - The `taskId` prop (already on MentionPill) provides the target

**Note**: The actual backend command handlers for virtual tag actions (e.g. `vtag.ready.start`) are implemented in the individual strategy cards (VT-5/6/7). This card only wires the frontend dispatch.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Right-click on virtual tag pill shows strategy-declared commands
- [ ] Commands dispatch to backend with task as target
- [ ] No entity commands (cut/copy/paste/archive) shown
- [ ] No `task.untag` shown
- [ ] Regular tag pills still show full entity context menu (no regression)
